title: The Design Leader: A Vision for Game Development in South Africa
summary: This paper examines the South African game development landscape and articulates a personal vision for design leadership within it. The first part maps the local industry, highlighting how it is dominated by micro-enterprises of one to five employees and shaped by tensions between creative innovation and infrastructural constraints such as load shedding, high data costs, and fragmented government support. Two studios serve as contrasting case studies - Free Lives, whose model centres on creative autonomy and titles like Broforce and Anger Foot, and Carry1st, a pan-African mobile publisher with over R500 million in funding targeting Sub-Saharan markets projected to reach R20 billion in value by 2025. The paper identifies a critical geographic imbalance, with 55.9% of studios concentrated in the Western Cape. The second part outlines a personal design leadership vision centred on building a decentralised network of game development incubators in township and rural areas, combining entrepreneurship, cultural storytelling, offline-friendly tools, and community-driven curriculum to democratise access and shift the industry's centre of gravity beyond Cape Town.
